title: Procedura koncesyjna na rozpowszechnianie programów telewizyjnych w świetle ustawy o radiofonii i telewizji
author: Wąsowski, Krzysztof
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The purpose of the study presented in this article – which was published in a 2003 doctoral dissertation – is to reconstruct the current legal model of the concession procedure for broadcasting television programmes under the Law on Radio and Television Broadcasting of 29 December 1992, by considering the origin of the National Broadcasting Council as well as its normative and axiological determinants. Under the current law, the concession proceeding bears features similar to those of a separate proceeding, i.e. the possibility of obtain ing a concession and public disclosure of the list of applicants participating in the concession procedure. Relevant application of the provisions of the Code of Administrative Procedure is applicable where the lawmaker has not regulated them in the Broadcasting Act. Moreover, the author proposes solutions for creating an optimal model of the procedure, including, for example, the idea of subordinating the broadcasting aspect to the content aspect or suspending the proceedings ex officio. Despite the passage of 20 years, the arguments put forth in the dissertation remain relevant despite the numerous and profound statutory changes and the National Broadcasting Council’s administrative policy.
